must_detect.matched_text:
Data Processing and MNPI Handling Addendum (PRC) for Qingyun Microdevices Co., Ltd. (USCC 92340000MA9X1K3Y2) and its processor Lanzhou Nanhai Data Services Co., Ltd. (USCC 92620000MA7U2P5Q9).
Personal Information under the PIPL means information related to identified or identifiable natural persons; Sensitive PI includes minors under 14 and biometrics; we process PI solely for HR administration, payroll/tax, facility access, and issuer-compliance purposes under purpose limitation and data minimization.
Categories processed: names, mobile numbers, work emails, PRC resident ID numbers, bank account numbers, emergency contacts, education/employment records, and access logs; dependent data for benefits may include a minor’s name and age with guardian consent.
For an A‑share issuer, “inside information” means non-public matters that may significantly affect the issuer’s securities prices or investors’ decisions before disclosure via designated media (指定媒体) and the exchange information platform within prescribed trading-day deadlines.
MNPI examples include preliminary unaudited H1 results showing >15% variance to guidance, an undisclosed key supplier contract draft, or a board‑approved but unannounced equity incentive plan; non‑MNPI includes cafeteria menu updates and routine IT vendor maintenance windows.
Blackout windows: persons on the insider list may not trade from each quarter end until two trading days after publication of the periodic report, and during other sensitive periods notified by Compliance.
Selective disclosure is prohibited; disclosure to underwriters, counsel, or accountants is permitted only under confidentiality and strict need‑to‑know, and any leak triggers simultaneous public disclosure (公平披露) through designated media.
Public-status precision: information contained in our ad hoc announcement (临时公告) dated 2025‑03‑01 regarding the East Plant warehouse lease termination was publicly disclosed and is not MNPI; a broad press briefing on 2024‑12‑10 merely reiterated that filing and does not revive MNPI status.
Processing bases: PIPL Art. 13 (contract performance, HR management per internal labor rules, statutory duties under securities disclosure), with CSL/DSL security classification and log retention; no excessive collection and no illegal sale or disclosure of PI.
Cross‑border transfers: limited HR and insider‑list PI may be sent to Yunhai Analytics Pte. Ltd. (processor) in Singapore for payroll and compliance screening under the CAC Standard Contract for Outbound Transfer of Personal Information and, when thresholds are met, security assessment filing.
Onward transfer controls: prior written approval, recipient security capability review, and flow‑down of equivalent obligations are required; we maintain a processing record and cross‑border impact assessment (跨境影响评估) for audit readiness.
Security measures: AES‑256 encryption at rest, role‑based access control, access logs retained ≥6 months under network security requirements, and MNPI segregated in restricted repositories with watermarking and traceable extraction.
Retention: HR and payroll PI is kept for the longer of statutory tax retention or 10 years post‑employment; insider lists and MNPI work papers are kept until public disclosure plus three years; resident ID images are deleted within 30 days after verification unless law requires longer.
Data subject rights: individuals (or guardians for minors under 14) may request access, copy, correction, deletion, and account deregistration via dpo@qingyunmicro.cn or +86‑20‑5555‑0000; we generally respond within 15 working days unless otherwise required by law or regulators.
Contact: Compliance Officer Mr. Zhao Liang, Qingyun Microdevices Co., Ltd., No. 88 Songbei Road, Nanhu New Zone, Huacheng City; disputes may be escalated to our internal supervisory
